windows.text: <Pages> Five years from now it is expected that over 90% of all digital content will be either fully or partially generated with the aid of artificial intelligence (AI). In a so-called synthetic society it is no longer possible to easily distinguish what is real or true and what is not. This open access book focuses on 4 leading technologies, all highly robotic in nature, that form the core of the new synthetic society: 1. <STORIES about the technology behind the increasingly popular humanoid robots and their applications, ranging from science and education to the clinical treatment of patients, from film and TV to art and from the virtual clothing store to the high-end bank. 2. LIES and hoaxes about the technology behind the deepfake-videos, which enable the production of completely realistic, yet false, videos and can as such influence any number of political and judicial proceedings as well as everyday communication and the work of all the media, such as current affairs shows, and other news outlets. 3. ILLUSIONS about the technology behind and applications of augmented reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR), all enabled by robotic techniques that affect ever more aspects of society, from games and tourism to fashion and health, and many more. To get a full grip on these technologies, their underlying technology is described and explored in three distinct contexts - in an educational environment for the benefit of students, in a therapeutic setting to benefit patients, and finally in an entertainment or creative environment to benefit society at large, and as many economic sectors as possible, from shops to banks. The book also discusses the negative uses of the underlying technologies - such as using AR for identity theft and fraud, deepfakes for fake news, pseudo-events or attacks on an individuals' privacy and virtual reality to blackmail or sexually exploit individuals. Such potential negative consequences and applications cannot be foreseen in advance, but are dealt with in a detailed societal reflection, offering various solutions in respect of the effects on our post-truth society, the privatisation of the public domain and, last but not least, the loss of individual and societal trust.
windows.label: Human Written

windows.text: In so doing the book evaluates which aspects of the current European legal paradigm apply to these new technologies and reflects in particular on privacy, data protection, fundamental rights, procedural law, tort law and the regulation of artificial intelligence, before presenting potential regulatory alternatives to deal with the current regulatory gaps and conundrums - with the message that that there are no easy solutions. The Ebook editions of this book are available under an open access Creative Commons licence, available on bloomsburycollections.com. For more information about the publication, including full tables of contents, author biographies and order forms, see: https://www.bloomsburycollections.com/cat/product/artificial-illusions
